cnWords: Rosa...我需要再核实一下你的陈述。事发当晚，你说你在哪里？
enWords: Rosa... I need to verify your statement again. The night of the incident, where did you say you were?

cnWords: 我...我告诉过你，我在打扫地下室走廊。从九点半开始，一直到...到很晚...
enWords: I... I told you, I was cleaning the basement corridor. Started at nine-thirty, until... until very late...

cnWords: 你的工作记录卡写得很清楚：11月15日，23:00到01:00，一楼走廊清洁。不是地下室。
enWords: Your work record card says it clearly: November 15th, 23:00 to 01:00, first floor corridor cleaning. Not the basement.

cnWords: 我...呃...我记错了...是的，一楼走廊...不是地下室...
enWords: I... um... I got it wrong... yes, the first floor corridor... not the basement...

cnWords: 一楼走廊。离接待室很近。你看到了什么？
enWords: The first floor corridor. Close to the reception room. What did you see?

cnWords: 什么都没看到！我只是在打扫...低着头擦地板...什么都没注意到...
enWords: Didn't see anything! I was just cleaning... head down scrubbing the floor... didn't notice anything...

cnWords: 你的储物间里有一条毛巾。闻起来像氯仿。
enWords: There's a towel in your storage room. It smells like chloroform.

cnWords: 那...那不是我的...我不知道那是什么...
enWords: That... that's not mine... I don't know what that is...

cnWords: 走廊垃圾桶里还有一个氯仿瓶，瓶口还是湿的。Rosa，你用氯仿把我迷晕了。
enWords: There's also a chloroform bottle in the corridor trash bin, still wet at the opening. Rosa, you knocked me out with chloroform.

cnWords: ...是的。是我。我用毛巾捂住了你的口鼻...对不起...真的对不起...
enWords: ...Yes. It was me. I used the towel to cover your mouth and nose... I'm sorry... I'm so sorry...

cnWords: ...为什么？
enWords: ... Why?

cnWords: 我女儿...她病了...病得很重...我没有钱...他说如果我帮忙，他就能帮我女儿...
enWords: My daughter... she's sick... very sick... I have no money... he said if I helped, he could help my daughter...
... Who?

cnWords: 是我...我一个人做的...我把你迷晕然后拖到接待室...我杀了Webb先生...
enWords: It was me... I did it alone... I knocked you out then dragged you to the reception room... I killed Mr. Webb...

cnWords: 走廊地板上的拖拽痕迹很深，至少有150磅的重量。Rosa，你体重不到120磅。你不可能拖动我。
enWords: The drag marks on the corridor floor are deep, at least 150 pounds. Rosa, you weigh less than 120 pounds. You couldn't have dragged me.

cnWords: 我...我用尽了全力...我...
enWords: I... I used all my strength... I...

cnWords: 你在替谁顶罪？
enWords: Who are you taking the fall for?

cnWords: ...我不能说...他会杀了我女儿...
enWords: ...I can't say... he'll kill my daughter...

cnWords: Rosa。我不是警察。我是来找真相的。你女儿需要一个能照顾她的母亲，不是一个替别人坐牢的母亲。
enWords: Rosa. I'm not the police. I'm here for the truth. Your daughter needs a mother who can take care of her, not a mother doing someone else's time in prison.

cnWords: ...是Morrison...Morrison警官。他...他让我帮忙把你迷晕...说他只是想问你几个问题...我不知道他会...会杀人...
enWords: ...It's Morrison... Officer Morrison. He... he asked me to help knock you out... said he just wanted to ask you some questions... I didn't know he would... would kill someone...

cnWords: 他是怎么威胁你的？
enWords: How did he threaten you?

cnWords: 他知道我女儿病了...说他认识医院的人...说他能让她免费治疗...但如果我不帮忙...他就会向移民局举报我...
enWords: He knew my daughter was sick... said he knew people at the hospital... said he could get her free treatment... but if I didn't help... he'd call immigration on me...

cnWords: 你把我迷晕之后发生了什么？
enWords: What happened after you knocked me out?

cnWords: Morrison来了...他把你拖进了接待室...我太害怕了，躲在储物间里不敢出来...
enWords: Morrison came... he dragged you into the reception room... I was so scared, I hid in the storage room and didn't dare come out...

cnWords: 谢谢你告诉我这些，Rosa。照顾好你的女儿。
enWords: Thank you for telling me this, Rosa. Take care of your daughter.

cnWords: Brennan先生...你...你不会告诉Morrison我说了这些，对吧...求你了...
enWords: Mr. Brennan... you... you won't tell Morrison I talked, will you... please...

cnWords: 我会处理Morrison的。你不用担心任何事。
enWords: I'll handle Morrison. You don't need to worry about anything.

cnWords: Morrison。
enWords: Morrison.

cnWords: Brennan。我办公室不欢迎嫌疑人。
enWords: Brennan. Suspects are not welcome in my office.

cnWords: 那我不会待太久。
enWords: Then I won't stay long.

cnWords: 我的报告写得很清楚。你不识字？
enWords: My report is clear. Can't you read?

cnWords: 我读过。写得不错。很专业，很详细，逻辑清晰，时间线完整——唯一的问题是，每一个关键细节都是精心设计的谎言。
enWords: I've read it. Well written. Professional, detailed, clear logic, complete timeline—the only problem is, every key detail is a carefully designed lie.

cnWords: 小心你的措辞，Brennan。诽谤警官可是重罪。现在，给我滚出去。
enWords: Watch your words, Brennan. Slandering an officer is a felony. Now, get out.

cnWords: 那我们就让证据说话。从你的时间线开始。
enWords: Then let's let the evidence speak. Starting with your timeline.

cnWords: 时间线？你他妈在我办公室里审问我？Brennan，我警告你，如果你不立刻滚出去，我会以妨碍公务罪逮捕你。
enWords: Timeline? Are you fucking interrogating me in my office? Brennan, I warn you, if you don't get out right now, I'll arrest you for obstruction of justice.

cnWords: 你可以试试。但那样的话，我就不得不把这些证据交给你的上司了。顺便说一句，我在Kirkland的时候，最擅长的就是整理证据链。
enWords: You can try. But then, I'd have to hand this evidence to your superior. By the way, at Kirkland, my specialty was organizing chains of evidence.

cnWords: 证据？你有什么证据？
enWords: Evidence? What evidence do you have?

cnWords: 很多。我们先聊聊你那晚的行踪。
enWords: A lot. Let's first talk about your whereabouts that night.

cnWords: 好。那我告诉你：11:30我接到匿名电话，立即出警。因为案情紧急，我一路飞车，最快速度赶到现场。中间没有耽误任何一秒。
enWords: Fine. I'll tell you: 11:30 I received an anonymous call and dispatched immediately. Because it was urgent, I drove as fast as possible. Didn't waste a single second.

cnWords: 最快速度？11:30出发，12:00到达。整整30分钟。但我查过地图，也问过Tommy，从你家到蓝月亮酒吧只需要15分钟。
enWords: Fastest speed? Departed 11:30, arrived 12:00. A full 30 minutes. But I checked the map and asked Tommy—from your house to Blue Moon takes only 15 minutes.

cnWords: 也许路上堵车！也许我记错了！这即使是法庭上也是允许的误差！
enWords: Maybe there was traffic! Maybe I misremembered! Such errors are admissible even in court!

cnWords: 深夜11点半的芝加哥会有这么严重的堵车？而且作为一名资深警探，你会把出警时间记错整整一倍？这可不是"误差"。
enWords: Serious traffic in Chicago at 11:30 PM? And as a veteran detective, you'd misremember your response time by double? That's no 'error'.

cnWords: 你到底想说什么？
enWords: What are you trying to say?

cnWords: 我想问那多出来的15分钟，你在哪里？在为了"最快速度"而特意绕路吗？或者...你在等我？等我先到，好实施你的计划？
enWords: I want to ask where you were for those extra 15 minutes. Taking a detour for 'fastest speed'? Or... were you waiting for me? Waiting for me to arrive first to execute your plan?

cnWords: 没有准备？那你怎么解释这份记录？便携式现场勘验箱。你亲笔签名的领用单。
enWords: No preparation? Then how do you explain this requisition record? Portable Field Investigation Kit. Signed by you.

cnWords: 那...那又能说明什么？警局每天都有物资领用！我作为一名警探，领一套设备补充库存有什么问题？
enWords: So... so what does that prove? Supplies are requisitioned every day! As a detective, what's wrong with restocking a kit?

cnWords: 补充库存？确实没问题。但问题在于时间。看看日期：11月2日，19:30。
enWords: Restocking? Sure, no problem. But the problem is the time. Look at the date: November 2nd, 19:30.

cnWords: 案发前一天的晚上，而且是你休假的时候。你会特意跑回警局，去领一套并不是标配的高级勘验箱来"补充库存"？
enWords: The night before the crime, and on your day off. You'd specifically run back to the station to 'restock' a non-standard, high-end kit?

cnWords: 我...我只是刚好路过！我做事喜欢未雨绸缪！不行吗？
enWords: I... I was just passing by! I like to be prepared! Is that a crime?

cnWords: 不，这不叫未雨绸缪。这叫"按图索骥"。你提前领了它，是因为你知道第二天晚上你会需要它。Morrison，这不是突发情况，这是预谋。
enWords: No, that's not preparedness. That's 'following the plan'. You took it early because you knew you'd need it the next night. Morrison, this wasn't an emergency, it was premeditated.

cnWords: 那是...那是巧合！我只是恰好把它扔在车上了。但我到了现场根本没用它！当时情况太乱，我只是做了目视检查！
enWords: That's... that's a coincidence! I just happened to toss it in the car. But I didn't use it at the scene! It was too chaotic, I only did a visual inspection!

cnWords: 没有使用？那么请解释一下这张照片。Webb办公室地板上有几个清晰的压痕。
enWords: Didn't use it? Then please explain this photo. Clear indentations on the floor of Webb's office.

cnWords: 压痕？那可能是椅子，或者是桌腿！这就是你想说的证据？几条划痕？
enWords: Indentations? Could be a chair, or table legs! This is your evidence? A few scratches?

cnWords: 仔细看。四方形分布，四角有特殊的防滑垫印迹。这不是家具留下的。
enWords: Look closely. Square distribution, special anti-slip pad marks at corners. Not furniture.

cnWords: 这些印迹完美契合那个勘验箱的底部。位置就在办公桌旁，你最方便"布置"现场的地方。
enWords: These marks perfectly match that kit's bottom. Right next to the desk, the most convenient spot for you to 'stage' the scene.

cnWords: 那可能是我进去的时候随手放了一下！我累了，把箱子放地上休息一下，有问题吗？
enWords: Maybe I set it down when I went in! I was tired, rested the box on the floor, any problem?

cnWords: 对！那肯定是我检查现场时留下的！我把箱子放在地上取证了！这很正常！我是警察，我在履行职责！
enWords: Yes! That must be from when I checked the scene! I put the box down to collect evidence! That's normal! I'm a cop, doing my duty!

cnWords: 履行职责？酒吧的员工们可不这么说。
enWords: Doing your duty? The bar staff would disagree.

cnWords: 他们？哪怕是这群臭虫也不可能时刻盯着我！我是后来才拿进去的！
enWords: Them? Even those vermin couldn't watch me every second! I brought it in later!

cnWords: 不，他们看得很清楚。你冲进房间时，两手空空，除了手电筒什么都没拿。你根本没带箱子进去。
enWords: No, they saw clearly. You rushed into the room empty-handed, except for a flashlight. You never brought the box in.

cnWords: 别编了。没人看到那个箱子。所以这些压痕只有一个解释：它们是你"提前"留下的。在所有人发现尸体之前，你就在那里布置现场了。
enWords: Stop lying. No one saw that box. So these marks have only one explanation: you left them 'in advance'. You were staging the scene before the body was even discovered.

cnWords: 多年的警探生活让你形成了肌肉记忆，哪怕是栽赃嫁祸，你都忘不了按部就班地使用工具。这个压痕，就是你留下的签名。
enWords: Years of detective work gave you muscle memory. Even when framing someone, you couldn't forget to use your tools methodically. This indentation is your signature.

cnWords: 够了...别说了...
enWords: Enough... stop...

cnWords: 这些证据足够把你送上法庭。现在，告诉我，是谁雇你的？对方给了你多少钱？
enWords: This evidence is enough to send you to court. Now, tell me, who hired you? How much did they pay?

cnWords: 钱？你以为我是为了钱？Brennan，你根本不懂...
enWords: Money? You think I did it for money? Brennan, you don't understand...

cnWords: 不然呢？
enWords: What else?

cnWords: 我欠疤面Tony五千美元。他要杀我全家。我老婆，Brennan。我他妈的老婆。
enWords: I owe Scarface Tony five thousand dollars. He's going to kill my family. My wife, Brennan. My fucking wife.

cnWords: 所以你选择栽赃我。
enWords: So you chose to frame me.

cnWords: 我已经走投无路了。有个神秘人找到我，直接给了我五千美金现金。他说这笔钱可以帮我还债，保护我家人，我只需要把你变成嫌疑人。但这不是我的错！
enWords: I was desperate. A mysterious man found me, gave me 5k cash right there. Said it would cover my debt, protect my family. All I had to do was make you a suspect. But it's not my fault!

cnWords: 多么慷慨的提议。
enWords: What a generous offer.

cnWords: 你不懂！你他妈根本不懂！如果我不这么做，他们会杀了我全家！
enWords: You don't understand! You fucking don't understand! If I didn't do it, they'd kill my whole family!

cnWords: 我懂。我只是不同意。
enWords: I understand. I just don't agree.

cnWords: 不同意？那你告诉我，Brennan，如果是你的家人，你会怎么做？
enWords: Don't agree? Then tell me, Brennan, if it were your family, what would you do?

cnWords: 我不知道。但我绝不会为了保全自己，就把枪口对准并肩作战的同伴。Morrison，你有选择。你只是因为恐惧，就跪下了。
enWords: I don't know. But I would never turn my gun on a partner just to save myself. Morrison, you had a choice. You just knelt down because of fear.

cnWords: 恐惧？哈...等你到了我这个岁数，有了软肋，你也会跪下的。那个人知道我的一切，如果不照做，明天芝加哥河里就会多几具尸体。我没有别的路可走！
enWords: Fear? Hah... when you get to my age, when you have weaknesses, you'll kneel too. That man knew everything about me. If I didn't do it, there'd be more bodies in the Chicago River by tomorrow. I had no other path!

cnWords: 这就是你所有的自我辩护？
enWords: Is that your entire defense?

cnWords: 这叫规则。要么被吃，要么喂别人吃。良心是有钱人的奢侈品。
enWords: It's the rules. Eat or be eaten. Conscience is a luxury for the rich.

cnWords: 也许。但我还是要试试。告诉我那个人是谁？
enWords: Maybe. But I'll still try. Tell me, who is he?

cnWords: 我...我为什么要告诉你？
enWords: Why... why should I tell you?

cnWords: 因为如果你不告诉我，我会把这些证据交给你的上司。到时候，你不仅会失去工作，还会面临法律制裁。但如果你配合，我可以尽量帮你。
enWords: Because if you don't, I'll give this evidence to your superior. You'll lose your job and face charges. If you help, I'll try to help you.

cnWords: 我...我可以告诉你...但你必须保证...保证不会牵连到我。
enWords: I... I can tell you... but you must promise... promise it won't implicate me.

cnWords: 我不能保证。但我可以保证，如果你配合，我会尽量帮你。
enWords: I can't promise that. But if you cooperate, I'll do what I can.

cnWords: 我只知道他叫Whale。他从不露面。只有纸条，特殊纹样的纸条。
enWords: I only know he's called Whale. Never shows his face. Only notes, with a special pattern.

cnWords: 除了你，他还收买了谁？
enWords: Besides you, who else?

cnWords: 我不知道...他说会有人配合...Webb是别人杀的，我只负责栽赃你...
enWords: I don't know... he said someone would cooperate... Webb was killed by someone else, I was just the setup man...

cnWords: 双重保险。
enWords: Double insurance.

cnWords: 对。他从一开始就没信任过我。Brennan...他还说了一件事。
enWords: Right. He never trusted me. Brennan... he said one more thing.

cnWords: 什么？
enWords: What?

cnWords: Webb约你去酒吧，不是要给你案子。他把你当成'保险侦探'。如果他出事，你一定会查到底。他利用了你的职业操守。
enWords: Webb didn't invite you to give you a case. You were his 'insurance detective'. If he died, you'd investigate. He used your professional integrity.

cnWords: 所以我也是工具。Webb的工具，Whale的目标。
enWords: So I'm a tool too. Webb's tool, Whale's target.

cnWords: 我们都是工具。Webb，我，你......每个人都是棋子。这个世界就是这样，Brennan。强者支配弱者，这是自然规律。我告诉你这些，不是因为别的...而是因为这对大家都好。继续对抗对谁都没好处。
enWords: We're all tools. Webb, me, you... everyone is a pawn. The world is like this, Brennan. The strong rule the weak, strict laws of nature. Give up, it's better for everyone.

cnWords: 不一样，Morrison。我被利用，但我没有出卖别人。你会为此付出代价。
enWords: Different, Morrison. I was used, but I didn't betray anyone. You'll pay for that.

cnWords: 代价？也许吧。但至少我现在还能保住我的位置。Brennan，我只是个执行者，真正的大人物在后面...你明白吗？
enWords: Pay? Maybe. But I keep my spot for now. Brennan, I'm just an executor. The big shots are behind... get it?

cnWords: 我当然明白。强者、弱者、执行者、工具......我在法庭、战壕和酒吧见过太多标签。但我更相信另外一种东西：有人总得站在真相那边。今天那个人是我。
enWords: I understand. Labels. But I believe someone has to stand for the truth. Today, that's me.

cnWords: 还有一件事。Webb的办公室，我需要进去看看。
enWords: One more thing. Webb's office—I need to take a look inside.

cnWords: 那个房间我也进不去。钥匙不在我这里。
enWords: I can't get into that room either. I don't have the key.

cnWords: 你是负责这个案子的警探。叫Tommy来开门。
enWords: You're the detective on this case. Get Tommy to open it.

cnWords: ......我可以打个电话。
enWords: ...I can make a call.

cnWords: 那就打吧。我在门口等你。
enWords: Then make it. I'll wait at the door.

cnWords: 顺便说一句，Morrison——下次想撒谎，记得把细节也练熟点。魅力靠的是自信，谎言靠的是细节。你两样都不够。
enWords: By the way, Morrison—next time you lie, practice the details. Charm needs confidence, lies need details. You lack both.

cnWords: Tommy，我们需要再谈谈Webb的生意。
enWords: Tommy, we need to talk about Webb's business again.

cnWords: Brennan先生...我该说的都说了...都是私酒生意...
enWords: Mr. Brennan... I've said everything... it's all bootlegging business...

cnWords: 是吗？那解释一下这个。你说收入全部来自酒类销售。但这份报告显示，Webb每月收入15,000美元。
enWords: Is that so? Then explain this. You said all income comes from alcohol sales. But this report shows Webb earns $15,000 monthly.

cnWords: 那...那份报告一定是弄错了...我...我没有紧张...Webb先生只做私酒生意，绝对没有其他业务！
enWords: That... that report must be wrong... I... I'm not nervous... Mr. Webb only does bootlegging, absolutely no other business!

cnWords: Tommy，你们的酒卖得不错。每月4,000美元，在禁酒令时代算是小康水平。可有个小问题——Webb每月收入是15,000。11,000美元的差额，你打算怎么解释？
enWords: Tommy, your liquor sells well. $4,000 a month. But there's a small problem—Webb's monthly income is $15,000. How do you explain the $11,000 difference?

cnWords: 那个...可能...可能是我算错了...
enWords: That... maybe... maybe I calculated it wrong...

cnWords: 算错？Tommy，我当过律师，最不怕的就是算账。11,000美元的差额，你做账三年，会'记错'一万多美元？
enWords: Calculated wrong? Tommy, I was a lawyer. The difference is $11,000. You've been keeping books for three years, you'd 'miscount' over ten thousand?

cnWords: 好吧...好吧...Webb先生还做...古董买卖...中国花瓶，欧洲油画...那些有钱人喜欢的东西...
enWords: Okay... okay... Mr. Webb also deals in... antiques... Chinese vases, European oil paintings... things rich people like...

cnWords: 古董。有意思。为什么一开始不说？
enWords: Antiques. Interesting. Why didn't you say so at first?

cnWords: 因为...因为那些客户都是体面人...Thompson议员，Coleman法官...Webb先生说要保密...不该问的不问...这是规矩...
enWords: Because... those clients are respectable people... Senator Thompson, Judge Coleman... Mr. Webb said to keep it secret... don't ask what shouldn't be asked... that's the rule...

cnWords: Tommy，你说中国花瓶卖给了Thompson议员，欧洲油画卖给了Coleman法官。对吗？
enWords: Tommy, you said the Chinese vase was sold to Senator Thompson, and the European painting to Judge Coleman. Correct?

cnWords: 对...对...都已经卖出去了...
enWords: Yes... yes... they're all sold...

cnWords: Tommy，我昨晚去Webb办公室拍了些照片。中国花瓶和欧洲油画，它们还在Webb办公室里。你是怎么卖'空气'的？
enWords: Tommy, I took some photos in Webb's office last night. The Chinese vase and the European painting are still there. How did you sell 'air'?

cnWords: 也许...也许Webb先生又买回来了...我...我只是按Webb先生的指示记账！他说这些是'特殊的商业安排'...让我记录成古董销售...
enWords: Maybe... maybe Mr. Webb bought them back... I... I just keep books according to Mr. Webb's instructions! He said these were 'special business arrangements'... asked me to record them as antique sales...

cnWords: '特殊的商业安排'——这话听起来像黑手党拿来的。Tommy，你到底在帮Webb做什么？
enWords: 'Special business arrangements'—sounds like something from the mob. Tommy, what exactly were you helping Webb with?

cnWords: 我不知道！我真的不知道！我不参与具体业务，只是做账...Webb先生从来不告诉我细节...
enWords: I don't know! I really don't know! I'm not involved in the business, just bookkeeping... Mr. Webb never tells me details...

cnWords: 这是你的工作日志。笔迹和你办公室的账本一模一样。'Thompson，访问30分钟，重点观看中国花瓶'。一个'只做账'的人，会记录客人看什么、看多久吗？
enWords: This is your work log. Handwriting matches the ledger in your office. 'Thompson, visited 30 mins, focused on Chinese vase'. Would a person 'just doing books' record what guests look at and for how long?

cnWords: 是...是Webb先生让我记的...
enWords: It... it was Mr. Webb who asked me to record it...

cnWords: 同一件古董被'卖'给同一个人多次。Tommy，那是按月收取的保护费。你管理着整个勒索网络的账目。每个客户，每笔交易，都在你这个'只做账的'脑子里。
enWords: The same antique 'sold' to the same person multiple times. Tommy, that's a monthly protection fee. You manage the accounts for the entire blackmail ring. Every client, every transaction, is in your head.

cnWords: 我没有选择！Webb先生说如果我不配合，就让我失业！我有家要养...我不能失去这份工作！
enWords: I had no choice! Mr. Webb said if I didn't cooperate, he'd fire me! I have a family to support... I can't lose this job!

cnWords: 所以你帮他勒索那些人。
enWords: So you helped him blackmail those people.

cnWords: 我知道这不对...但我能怎么办？我只是个打工的...我没有Webb的手段...
enWords: I know it's wrong... but what could I do? I'm just an employee... I don't have Webb's means...

cnWords: Tommy，你会为此付出代价。但如果你配合调查...至少不会像Webb那样。
enWords: Tommy, you will pay for this. But if you cooperate... at least you won't end up like Webb.

cnWords: 我...我会配合...我什么都说...那些不是真正的古董销售...每个古董就是一个档案的代号...中国花瓶代表Thompson议员，欧洲油画代表Coleman法官...
enWords: I... I will cooperate... I'll say everything... those weren't real antique sales... each antique was a code for a file... Chinese vase for Senator Thompson, European painting for Judge Coleman...

cnWords: 五年。你做这个，做了整整五年。Webb被杀的那晚，你在家陪孩子。那些被勒索的人绝望自杀的时候——你也在陪孩子。你是个好父亲，但那些被你毁掉的家庭里，也有父亲。
enWords: Five years. You've done this for five years. The night Webb was killed, you were home with your kids. When those blackmailed people committed suicide in despair—you were also with your kids. You're a good father, but in those families you destroyed, there were fathers too.

cnWords: 我...我知道错了...我会付出代价的...
enWords: I... I know I was wrong... I will pay the price...

cnWords: 现在告诉我——Webb最近勒索的那个人，是谁？
enWords: Now tell me—who was Webb blackmailing recently?

cnWords: 我...我真的不知道他的名字...Webb先生从来不说...
enWords: I... I really don't know his name... Mr. Webb never said...

cnWords: 但你知道Webb遇到麻烦了。
enWords: But you knew Webb was in trouble.

cnWords: 对...他最近...很不对劲...那种紧张我以前从没见过...
enWords: Yes... he was... very off recently... a kind of nervousness I've never seen before...

cnWords: 多不对劲？
enWords: How off?

cnWords: 他...他开始锁门。以前Webb先生从不锁门，现在连去趟厕所都要把办公室锁上。还有...他开始备枪。Brennan先生，他真的很害怕。
enWords: He... he started locking the door. Mr. Webb never locked his door before, now he locks it even to go to the toilet. And... he started carrying a gun. Mr. Brennan, he was really scared.

cnWords: Webb害怕。有意思。一个靠威胁别人活了五年的人，终于遇到更可怕的了。
enWords: Webb scared. Interesting. A man who lived by threatening others for five years finally met something scarier.

cnWords: 如果连Webb先生都害怕...那说明对方真的...真的很危险...
enWords: If even Mr. Webb was scared... that means the person is... really dangerous...

cnWords: 除了你，还有谁了解Webb的生意？
enWords: Besides you, who else knows about Webb's business?

cnWords: James...厨师James...
enWords: James... James the cook...

cnWords: 厨师？账本上有他的记录吗？
enWords: The cook? Any records of him in the ledger?

cnWords: James的工资高的离谱...Webb先生嘱咐我加的...应该是有什么问题...
enWords: James's salary is ridiculously high... Mr. Webb instructed me to add it... There must be something wrong...

cnWords: 高多少？
enWords: How much higher?

cnWords: 一个月500美金...其他厨师只有120...就算是主厨，也高得离谱...我问过Webb先生，他说不用管，照做就行...
enWords: $500 a month... other cooks only get $120... even for a head chef, it's ridiculously high... I asked Mr. Webb, he said don't worry, just do as told...

cnWords: 4倍工资。一个厨师拿这么多钱。你确定James只是厨师？
enWords: 4 times the salary. A cook getting paid this much. Are you sure James is just a cook?

cnWords: Brennan先生...我能说的都说了...剩下的，您得去问James...
enWords: Mr. Brennan... I've said all I can... for the rest, you have to ask James...

cnWords: James，我们聊聊。
enWords: James, let's talk.

cnWords: 正在忙。厨房还有活。
enWords: Busy. Still work in kitchen.

cnWords: Webb死了。你不想说点什么吗？
enWords: Webb is dead. Don't you want to say something?

cnWords: 我是优秀员工。我有奖状，还有推荐信...我自己写的，但是真的。我每天努力工作，忠诚，守规矩。我没做过任何坏事。
enWords: I am an excellent employee. I have awards, recommendation letters... wrote them myself, but true. I work hard every day, loyal, follow rules. Never did anything bad.

cnWords: 忠诚的员工？没做过坏事？那三天前发生的事怎么解释？
enWords: Loyal employee? Never did bad? Then explain what happened three days ago?

cnWords: Tommy亲眼看见你从Webb办公室出来，怀里抱着东西，还要躲着他。优秀员工会偷老板的东西吗？
enWords: Tommy saw you coming out of Webb's office, carrying something, hiding from him. Do excellent employees steal from their boss?

cnWords: 那...那不是偷...拿了...以为...不要的...那是垃圾...堆在角落...有灰...
enWords: That... that wasn't stealing... took it... thought... unwanted... garbage... in corner... dusty...

cnWords: 我不知道那是古董。我只是觉得...虽然破...但洗干净能给Anna插花。
enWords: I didn't know it was an antique. I just thought... broken as it is... cleaned up, Anna could use it for flowers.

cnWords: 你说你不知道它的价值？那你怎么解释这些？
enWords: You say you didn't know its value? Then explain these.

cnWords: 这张照片是在你家里发现的，你专门把这个'垃圾'拍照留念？Webb办公室还有它的鉴定报告——Bennington陶壶，价值两千美元。你天天在这工作，会不知道？
enWords: This photo was found at your home. You took a picture of this 'garbage' as a keepsake? Webb's office also has its appraisal report - Bennington Jug, worth $2000. You work here every day, and you didn't know?

cnWords: Anna...快生了。家里...没钱。申请身份...被拒了...需要钱...
enWords: Anna... is due soon. No money... at home. Citizenship application... rejected... need money...

cnWords: 所以你为了钱背叛了Webb？
enWords: So you betrayed Webb for money?

cnWords: 不！我没有背叛！Webb对我很好...送书给我...教我英语...
enWords: No! I didn't betray him! Webb was good to me... gave me books... taught me English...

cnWords: 也就是偷了这个壶...我没想害他。我们关系很好。我发誓！我一直把他当恩人！
enWords: Just stole this jug... didn't mean to harm him. We were good. I swear! Always saw him as a benefactor!

cnWords: 恩人？那Vivian听到的是什么？
enWords: Benefactor? Then what did Vivian hear?

cnWords: Vivian亲耳听到你在Webb办公室里大吼——'别接那单，会出大事的'。你不是在和你的恩人叙旧，你是在和他争吵。而且是非常激烈的争吵。
enWords: Vivian heard you screaming in Webb's office—'Don't take that deal, it'll cause big trouble'. You weren't catching up with your benefactor, you were arguing. A very heated argument.

cnWords: 您...怎么知道...
enWords: How... how do you know...

cnWords: Webb死了。正如你所预料的那样。你那时候就知道他会死，因为你知道'Whale'是谁。
enWords: Webb is dead. Just as you predicted. You knew back then he would die, because you know who 'Whale' is.

cnWords: 别说那个名字...求您...别说了...
enWords: Don't say that name... Please... stop...

cnWords: 你在保护谁？Anna？
enWords: Who are you protecting? Anna?

cnWords: 我保护不了任何人！Webb以为有证据就安全了，结果他死了！Morrison就在门外，却什么都'没看见'！
enWords: I can't protect anyone! Webb thought evidence made him safe, but he died! Morrison was right outside and 'saw nothing'!

cnWords: 'Whale'的人到处都是...警察、法官、银行家...我只是个厨师...我能做什么？我只想带Anna走...
enWords: 'Whale's' people are everywhere... police, judges, bankers... I'm just a cook... what can I do? I just want to take Anna away...

cnWords: 你知道'Whale'是谁吗？
enWords: Do you know who 'Whale' is?

cnWords: 只知道名字...Webb说这是'最大的一笔生意'...做成了就一辈子不愁...我求他别做...会死人...
enWords: Only know the name... Webb said it was 'the biggest deal'... set for life if done... I begged him not to... people will die...

cnWords: 求您了...别问了...如果是为了Anna，让我走吧...
enWords: Please... stop asking... if for Anna's sake, let me go...

cnWords: 回厨房去吧。把今天的活干完。
enWords: Go back to the kitchen. Finish your work for today.

cnWords: 谢谢...谢谢...我一定会干完的...
enWords: Thank you... thank you... I'll finish it...

cnWords: 但别试图逃跑。你逃不掉的。
enWords: But don't try to run. You can't escape.

cnWords: 我知道...我不会跑...
enWords: I know... I won't run...

cnWords: Vivian，我得问你几个问题。
enWords: Vivian, I have a few questions.

cnWords: 律师要在旁边吗？我不过是唱歌的，Brennan先生。
enWords: Do I need a lawyer? I'm just a singer, Mr. Brennan.

cnWords: 通常唱歌的不会这副表情——看起来像是刚卸下了一个巨大的包袱。
enWords: Singers don't usually look like they just unloaded a huge burden.

cnWords: 你在胡说什么？我只是刚结束演出，很累。
enWords: What are you talking about? I'm just tired after the show.

cnWords: 别跟我演戏。回答问题：你和Webb现在到底是什么关系？
enWords: Don't act with me. Answer the question: What is your relationship with Webb now?

cnWords: 曾经他出钱，我出声线，仅此而已。我依然深爱着他，他的死让我很伤心。
enWords: He paid, I sang. That's all. I still love him deeply, his death broke me.

cnWords: "深爱"？这封信可不是这么说的。"已无法忍受"、"彻底结束"... 这些字眼看起来不像是情书，更像是分手信。
enWords: 'Deep love'? This letter says otherwise. 'Unbearable', 'Completely over'... These words look more like a breakup letter than a love letter.

cnWords: 每个人情绪失控的时候都会写点蠢东西。那封信我从来没打算给他看，我就随手扔在废纸篓里了。
enWords: Everyone writes stupid things when emotional. I never planned to show him. Just tossed it in the bin.

cnWords: 只有废纸篓？我在你的梳妆台上发现的，折叠得很整齐。如果你真的只是发泄情绪，为什么不把它撕了或者烧了？这看起来像是在犹豫要不要寄出去。
enWords: Just the bin? Found it on your dresser, neatly folded. If just venting, why not tear or burn it? Looks like hesitation to send.

cnWords: 你不懂女人，侦探。有些东西留着是为了提醒自己......提醒自己忍耐是有极限的。但这不代表我不爱他了。
enWords: You don't understand women. Kept it as a reminder... that patience has limits. Doesn't mean I don't love him.

cnWords: 忍耐？你说Webb向你承诺过什么？
enWords: Patience? What did Webb promise you?

cnWords: 他承诺带我离开这一行。我们和好了，信只是过去式。我们甚至已经预订了下周去法国的船票，打算去那里重新开始。
enWords: Promised to take me away. We reconciled, letter is past. Even booked tickets to France for next week. Fresh start.

cnWords: 如果计划还在，为什么我在你的垃圾桶里找到了被撕碎的船票？
enWords: If the plan stands, why did I find torn ticket scraps in your trash?

cnWords: 撕票说明旅行取消，不说明我杀了他。
enWords: Torn means cancelled, doesn't mean I killed him.

cnWords: 你还写过'谁背叛我，我就让他痛不欲生'。
enWords: You also wrote 'Whoever betrays me, I will make them suffer'.

cnWords: 日记。你在我最糟糕的一天里偷听我脑子里的脏话，这很英勇？
enWords: Diary. Eavesdropping on my dark thoughts on my worst day. Heroic?

cnWords: 我撕掉船票是因为吵架了。但这不意味着我们要分手！这只是情侣间的正常摩擦。我们决定和平解决分歧，好聚好散。没有仇恨。
enWords: Tore ticket because of fight. Doesn't mean breakup! Just lovers' quarrel. We decided to solve peacefully. No hate.

cnWords: 和平解决？Tommy告诉我Webb最近和Rita打得火热。这就是你所谓的"没有仇恨"的根源吧？背叛。
enWords: Peaceful? Tommy told me Webb's been hot with Rita lately. That's the root of your 'no hate', isn't it? Betrayal.

cnWords: Webb换投资项目而已。他出钱买下我，我给他利润。这骗局从第一天就存在，我并不惊讶。
enWords: Webb just changing investments. He bought me, I gave profit. Scam since day one, not surprised.

cnWords: Tommy说他看到你站在走廊，看着他们。
enWords: Tommy saw you in hallway, watching them.

cnWords: 是...我看到了。那天我在走廊看到他和Rita...他对她笑的样子...看起来那么温柔。我站在那里，像个多余的人。
enWords: Yes... I saw. Him and Rita... his smile... so gentle. I stood there, like a spare part.

cnWords: 但这又能说明什么？他不欠我什么。我们只是交易关系。我早就知道了，不是吗？
enWords: But what does it prove? He owes me nothing. Just business. I knew it, didn't I?

cnWords: 那你为什么说'我为他做了那么多'？
enWords: Then why did you say 'I did so much for him'?

cnWords: 因为我把一生抵押给他，换来的只是一张过期船票。这叫投入产出比，Brennan先生。不是什么廉价的感情故事。
enWords: Because I mortgaged my life to him, for an expired ticket. That's ROI, Mr. Brennan. Not a cheap romance.

cnWords: 案发夜11:10，有人看到你去会客室。
enWords: Murder night, 11:10, someone saw you at parlor.

cnWords: 我确实去了门口。敲门，他在里面吼'别打扰我'。门锁着，我没见到他，就...离开了。
enWords: I went. Knocked, he shouted 'Don't disturb me'. Locked. Didn't see him, so... I left.

cnWords: 你恨他。
enWords: You hate him.

cnWords: 恨过。也想过——如果他死了，一切会不会干净一些。但那只是在脑子里走了一遍。每个被背叛的女人都这么想过。
enWords: Hated him. Thought about him dying. But only in my head. Every betrayed woman thinks that.

cnWords: 最后一次接触就是那句'别打扰我'？
enWords: Last contact was 'Don't disturb me'?

cnWords: 是。11点10分，会客室门口。我被他像赶走一只流浪狗一样赶走。然后我转身，离开。之后发生什么，不关我的事。
enWords: Yes. 11:10. Chased away like a stray dog. Turned and left. Can't care about what happened next.

cnWords: 你坚持没杀Webb。
enWords: You insist you didn't kill him.

cnWords: 我承认我恨他，承认我幻想过他死，承认我为这个幻想设计过完美的计划。但我没有动手。你想要的供词——一个杀人犯的供词——我没有。
enWords: Admit hate, fantasy, plan. But no action. The confession you want - murderer's confession - I don't have.

cnWords: 我只是一个...被背叛的傻女人。
enWords: I'm just a... betrayed stupid woman.

cnWords: Vivian，等一下。我需要确认一些事。这可能会改变你对Webb的看法。Webb留给你的便条：'保险箱密码是我们开始的那一天。那一刻是真的。'
enWords: Vivian, wait. Need to confirm something. Might change your view. Webb's note: 'Safe code is day we started. That moment was real.'

cnWords: 他骗人...一直都在骗人...
enWords: Liar... always lying...

cnWords: Tommy告诉我，Webb知道Rita的真实身份。他故意和Rita走近，是为了保护你。Webb和Rita的关系是假的。
enWords: Tommy says Webb knew Rita's identity. Got close to protect you. Relationship was fake.

cnWords: 我以为...我以为他背叛了我...我看到的那些...原来都是他演给'Whale'看的...
enWords: I thought... I thought he betrayed me... What I saw... all an act for 'Whale'...

cnWords: 还有这个项链。'给我最爱的Vivian，永远的舞台。'
enWords: And this necklace. 'To my dearest Vivian, forever stage.'

cnWords: 那是谎言。Webb从来没有兑现过任何承诺。
enWords: That's a lie. Webb never kept promises.

cnWords: 也许不全是谎言。便条上写：'密码是我们开始的那一天。那一刻是真的。'
enWords: Maybe not all lies. Note says: 'Code is start day. Moment was real.'

cnWords: 那个时刻是什么时候？
enWords: When was that moment?
1920. August 7th.

cnWords: 发生了什么？
enWords: What happened?

cnWords: 那年我17岁，父母把我卖给妓院。在路上...Webb出现了，把我带到了蓝月亮。8月7日那天，我第一次在舞台上唱歌。
enWords: 17. Parents sold me to brothel. Webb appeared on the way, took me to Blue Moon. Aug 7th, first time singing on stage.

cnWords: 那一刻...我以为自己终于不是商品了。我以为Webb和其他男人不一样。
enWords: That moment... thought I wasn't a commodity anymore. Thought Webb was different.

cnWords: 但酒吧开了不到半年，他就开始勒索客人。而我...我负责陪那些VIP，套取秘密。我以为的'舞台'，不过是另一种牢笼。
enWords: But half year later, he blackmailed guests. I... I accompanied VIPs, stole secrets. 'Stage' was just another cage.

cnWords: 但他还记得那个时刻。否则不会用那个日期做密码。
enWords: But he remembered. Or he wouldn't use that date as code.

cnWords: 可那又怎么样？他把我从一个牢笼带到另一个牢笼。
enWords: So what? He took me from one cage to another.

cnWords: 你说得对。但至少...那个时刻可能是真的。
enWords: You're right. But at least... that moment might have been real.

cnWords: 0807。1920年8月7日。那是他人生中唯一值得记住的时刻。现在你可以走了。
enWords: 0807. Aug 7th 1920. Only moment worth remembering. Now you can leave.

cnWords: James。
enWords: James.

cnWords: 我知道！没杀人。11点半...枪响。大家都听到了。我在厨房，有人证。
enWords: I know! No kill. 11:30... gunshot. Everyone hear. I in kitchen. Witness.

cnWords: 11点半。你怎么这么确定时间？
enWords: 11:30. How are you so sure about the time?

cnWords: 大家...大家都听到了。问谁都一样。
enWords: Everyone... everyone heard. Ask anyone.

cnWords: 枪声从哪个方向传来的？
enWords: Which direction did the gunshot come from?

cnWords: Webb的...Webb的办公室。楼下。
enWords: Webb's... Webb's office. Downstairs.

cnWords: 你确定？
enWords: You sure?

cnWords: 我...确定。为什么这样问？
enWords: I... sure. Why ask like that?

cnWords: 因为我去看了Webb办公室的窗户。
enWords: Because I checked the window in Webb's office.
Window...?

cnWords: 看这个弹孔。是从里往外的。凶手在房间里开了一枪打碎窗户，就是为了让你们以为那是作案时间。
enWords: Look at this bullet hole. From inside out. The killer shot through the window from inside, just to make everyone think that was the time of death.

cnWords: 这...这不能说明什么。也许...也许有人在外面开枪？
enWords: This... proves nothing. Maybe... someone shot from outside?

cnWords: 弹道方向不会说谎，James。11点半的枪声，是伪造的。
enWords: Ballistics don't lie, James. The 11:30 gunshot was faked.

cnWords: 不可能！如果有枪声...能听到。酒吧很小。藏不住。
enWords: Impossible! If gunshot... we hear. Bar small. Cannot hide.

cnWords: 是吗？当晚11点的时候，你在哪？
enWords: Really? Where were you at 11 that night?

cnWords: 厨房...我一直在厨房。
enWords: Kitchen... I was in kitchen.

cnWords: 11点到11点10分，舞台在演什么节目？
enWords: 11:00 to 11:10, what was playing on stage?

cnWords: 我...不记得。很多节目。
enWords: I... don't remember. Many shows.

cnWords: 《芝加哥狂想》。Rosa说那晚的鼓点震耳欲聋。
enWords: 'Chicago Rhapsody'. Rosa said the drums were deafening that night.

cnWords: 所以...所以什么？
enWords: So... so what?

cnWords: 这个开场曲是Vivian安排的。但你利用了它。在最强鼓点的时候，就算开枪也不会被听到。
enWords: Vivian arranged this opener. But you exploited it. During the loudest drums, even a gunshot wouldn't be heard.

cnWords: 你...你在说什么...我听不懂...
enWords: You... what are you saying... I don't understand...

cnWords: 你听得懂，James。11点，真正的枪声。11点半，假的枪声。
enWords: You understand, James. 11:00, the real shot. 11:30, the fake one.

cnWords: 就算音乐...但Vivian能证明！11点...她敲门了。听到了Webb的声音！
enWords: Even if music... but Vivian prove! 11:00... she knock. Hear Webb voice!

cnWords: Vivian。对，她确实敲门了。11点05分。
enWords: Vivian. Yes, she did knock. 11:05.

cnWords: 对！她听到Webb说话了！Webb那时候还活着！
enWords: Yes! She heard Webb talk! Webb was alive then!

cnWords: Vivian说那个声音'很奇怪'。是隔着门听到的。
enWords: Vivian said that voice was 'strange'. Heard through the door.
Strange...?

cnWords: 因为那不是Webb的声音。是你模仿的。
enWords: Because that wasn't Webb's voice. You mimicked it.

cnWords: 不...11点的时候我在和Tom一起送餐！他可以作证！
enWords: No... At 11, I was delivering food with Tom! He can testify!

cnWords: 送餐记录显示当时送餐只有你自己一个人。你就是这段时间来到Webb的办公室杀害了他。
enWords: The delivery log shows you were alone. That's when you went to Webb's office and killed him.

cnWords: 11点，你杀了他。11点05分，Vivian敲门，你锁着门，模仿他的声音让她离开。11点30分，你制造假枪声。
enWords: 11:00, you killed him. 11:05, Vivian knocked, you locked door, mimicked him to make her leave. 11:30, you faked the shot.

cnWords: 不...为什么？没有杀他的理由！他对我有恩！
enWords: No... Why? No reason to kill him! He good to me!

cnWords: 有恩？那这是什么？
enWords: Good to you? Then what's this?
This... this...

cnWords: 11月1日。Whale的那笔5000美金。Webb没收到。
enWords: November 1st. That $5000 from Whale. Webb didn't get it.

cnWords: 那是...那是别的钱...
enWords: That was... other money...

cnWords: 那是给你的杀人定金。你为了钱背叛了Webb，这也成了你给Whale的投名状。
enWords: That was your deposit for murder. You betrayed Webb for money. That became your ticket to Whale.

cnWords: 不...不是这样的...
enWords: No... not like that...

cnWords: 而且...你还帮Whale清理了Webb的保险箱。Webb把关键证据——录音、文件——都锁在里面。案发后都被烧毁了。
enWords: And... you cleaned Webb's safe for Whale. Webb locked evidence inside - recordings, files. All burnt after the murder.

cnWords: 只有你有机会拿到密码。James。
enWords: Only you could get the code. James.

cnWords: ……都是真的。
enWords: ...All true.

cnWords: 为什么？
enWords: Why?

cnWords: Whale...他找到我。说Webb要勒索他。
enWords: Whale... he found me. Said Webb was blackmailing him.

cnWords: 他说...如果我杀了Webb，他给我钱...很多钱...还能帮我和Anna拿到合法身份...
enWords: He said... if I kill Webb, he gives money... lots of money... and help me and Anna get legal status...

cnWords: 我不想一辈子躲在阴影里...我的孩子...我不想他一出生就是非法移民...
enWords: I don't want to hide in shadows forever... my child... I don't want him born illegal immigrant...

cnWords: 跟我回警局。指证Whale。你和Anna可以进证人保护。
enWords: Come to station. Testify against Whale. You and Anna can get witness protection.

cnWords: 保护？警局...全是Whale的人。Morrison就是。
enWords: Protection? Police... full of Whale's men. Morrison is one.

cnWords: 我可以——
enWords: I can—

cnWords: 你保护不了我们。如果我被抓...Whale会对Anna下手。我的孩子...
enWords: You can't protect us. If I'm caught... Whale will hurt Anna. My child...

cnWords: James...你在想什么？
enWords: James... what are you thinking?

cnWords: 我的孩子需要的是一个好父亲...不是一个杀人犯。
enWords: My child needs... a good father... not a murderer.

cnWords: James，别——
enWords: James, don't—

cnWords: 这是我唯一能保护他们的方式了。
enWords: This is the only way I can protect them now.

这是物证，Morrison。弹壳口径和 Vivian 的枪对不上——
把那个给我。
这是证据——
现在是我的了。
你没有权力——
O'Malley。你今晚在这里记的那些东西——我建议你考虑清楚准备发表什么。

Vivian 有杀人动机，有杀人计划，她本人也说过自己就是凶手。她的手枪就是凶器。
那个协议书——Webb 委托我调查的协议书——
Webb 签的。Webb 死了。72 小时也过了，Brennan。
这个协议书是——
把她带出来。
你没有足够的证据，Morrison——
我有足够的动机。我有足够的口供。法庭会决定剩下的。
Vivian——她不是凶手，你手里那颗弹壳就是证明，你清楚——
案件已经结案。

动作先于语言：弹壳被收进口袋、协议书被折好放进外套，这两个动作都在相关台词之前完成，或与台词同步，没有"说完了再做"的回旋余地。
宣告而非辩论：Morrison 说"Vivian 有动机、有计划、手枪是凶器"时用的语气注释是"不是辩论"，是宣告——这在演出层面关掉了玩家期待的"对峙"节奏，直接切入"已经决定了"的事后状态。

压制 Emma 用的是权威+冷漠：记者证我压得住，报道我也压得住，语气不提高，不愤怒，就是在告知一件他有能力做到的事。这种冷静比愤怒更有压迫感，因为它意味着"这对我来说不费力"。
压制 Zack 用的是程序+终局：协议是 Webb 签的，Webb 死了，72 小时也过了。Morrison 用的是 Zack 自己那个框架里的规则来关掉 Zack 的调查——他没有跟 Zack 争证据，他直接宣布那个让 Zack 调查合法的前提不存在了。这种压制更彻底，因为它不是力量压制，而是框架清除。

第二层是提问前的停顿。804003004 中，Morrison 转过头"看他，眼神和说话方式都很随意——但随意里有什么在盘算"，然后才问。这个停顿是审视的瞬间，不是思考的停顿。
第三层是回答的处理方式。Zack 每次回答都很短，Morrison 每次都"点了点头"然后继续——他记住了，但他没有说他在记。

骤变在 804003028——弹壳推到吧台上之后，Morrison 的视线落上去，然后是一个完全的静默节点。

骤变和"普通惊讶"的区别：普通惊讶会问问题（从哪来的、谁确认的型号）。Morrison 一个问题都没问，他直接伸手要拿弹壳。这个跳过"问问题"的反应本身就是那个"认出了某种东西"的表演。
